extraction.one_line_summary: RUBIO Einer won from the breakaway with a final 300m sprint after TIBOPINO attacked repeatedly on the final climb but couldn't sustain his aggression
tactical_patterns: TIBOPINO made excessive attacks on final climb but got counterproductive fatigue, losing sprint after dominating tactics earlier.
CEPEDA Alexander stayed in break despite teammate CARTHY catching from behind, revealing EF's willingness to split team commitments.
FORTUNATO Lorenzo attacked first from GC group but CARTHY dropped him easily, showing altitude-specific weakness on Swiss climbs.
SWIFT Ben performed exceptional domestique work on all terrain types for INEOS, enabling conservative Rosa group control.

rider_signals.RUBIO Einer: positive: sprinted strongest from reduced break after conserving energy while Tibopino overcommitted on attacks
rider_signals.TIBOPINO Giulio: negative: tactical overreach with excessive attacks yielded no gap but drained sprint power in final 300m
rider_signals.LEKNERSON Andreas: negative: lost 7 seconds to main GC rivals despite being in Rosa group, first visible weakness
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: bridged to break but couldn't drop Fortunato convincingly, suggesting form ceiling outside top-5
rider_signals.SWIFT Ben: positive: unexpectedly strong climbing for heavy domestique, key to INEOS controlling without Thomas spending matches
